calendar.matters:
Petition For Approval Of Accountings And For Their Settlement, Approval Of Trustee Fees; Approval Of Reformation Of Trust, Order Transferring Record Title To Real Property, And Confirmation Of Trusteeship
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Levin and Naznin Challa for Natalie Drees (Petitioner).

Ms. Challa stated that Edwin Lingsch, Jr. has not been contacted, that she has spoken with Natalie and Marlys (Edwin's Sister) who attempted to locate papers, that they contacted Ms. Lacoco Trustee of the Special Needs Trust and that Ms. Lacoco has not heard from him. Ms. Levin explained that Edwin is not to receive from the Mission Street property, that he has received everything that he is going to receive and that he will receive nothing more from Trust A. Ms. Levin also stated that the Mission Street property goes to her Client, that Edwin did receive the petition for declaratory relief which had a copy of this petition attached and that Edwin received the order on the declaratory relief. The Court pointed out that the Trustee fees in Trust C appear to be too low and the Trustee fees in Trust B appear to be too high. Ms. Levin clarified that Trustee fees for Trust C should be $7,620.21 and $2,454.47 for Trust B. Ms. Levin also stated that they do not want to re-notice. The Petition for Approval of Accountings, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Declaratory Judgement
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Levin and Naznin Challa for Natalie Drees (Petitioner).

The petition for declaratory judgment finding that the proposed petition does not violate the no contest clause of the trust is granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Declaratory Judgement
Off Calendar Apr-02-2014 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The petition for declaratory judgment is continued to April 16, 2014 in Dept. 204, at 9:00 a.m. due to clerical error.
